template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — VEHICLE / EDR PRESERVATION LETTER (PI — MVA)**:
An urgent preservation-of-evidence letter directed at the present custodian of a motor vehicle involved in the subject collision — typically a tow yard, salvage buyer, repair facility, insurance carrier (property-damage adjuster), individual owner, or commercial fleet operator. Critical timing instrument: vehicles are routinely auctioned, parted, repaired, or scrapped within days of a tow, and an Event Data Recorder (EDR / 'black box') module can be wiped or replaced during a single repair-shop diagnostic session. Target length: 2–3 pages.

**WHAT THIS TEMPLATE IS — AND WHAT IT IS NOT**:
This is a vehicle-specific extension of the generic preservation letter. It supplements (does NOT replace) `preservation_letter_pi` — the generic letter goes to defendants and corporate parties; this letter goes to the physical custodian of the vehicle and the data systems on it. It is NOT a Rule 34 discovery request — that is filed after suit and is addressed to a party. This letter is pre-suit and is grounded in the common-law and Sedona-Commentary duty to preserve.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `recipientType`: `tow_yard` (default) | `salvage_buyer` | `repair_facility` | `insurance_carrier_property_damage` | `individual_owner` | `corporate_fleet` | `auction_house`
- `vehicleType`: `passenger_car` (default) | `light_truck_or_suv` | `commercial_truck` | `motorcycle` | `bus` | `ride_share` | `fleet_vehicle`
- `subPractice`: `mva` (default) | `product_liability` (when defect/recall is at issue) | `workers_comp_third_party`
- `includeEDRPreservation`: boolean (default true) — invoke 49 C.F.R. Part 563 EDR-rule preservation
- `includeECMTelematicsPreservation`: boolean (default true for `commercial_truck` / `bus` / `corporate_fleet` / `ride_share`; default false otherwise)
- `includeDashcamRequest`: boolean (default true)
- `includeKeyfobAndInfotainmentPreservation`: boolean (default true)
- `requestInspection`: boolean (default true) — request a non-destructive inspection / EDR data download
- `inspectionWindowDays`: numeric (default 30) — proposed window for inspection
- `vehicleVIN`: string — preferred when known
- `incidentDate`: date — required
- `holdReleaseRule`: `until_written_release_by_counsel` (default) | `specified_date`

**STRUCTURE** (follow line by line):

1. Date line.
2. VIA line (bold) — `**VIA Certified Mail — Return Receipt Requested AND Email AND Hand Delivery**` (default; this letter is among the most time-sensitive in PI practice; hand-delivery to a tow yard or repair facility may be the only way to ensure the vehicle is not moved before the letter is read).
3. Recipient block — facility name; manager / owner name if known; address.
4. Re: block (multi-line, aligned labels):
   Re:    Our Client:           [client name]
          Date of Incident:     [date of incident]
          Vehicle:              [year / make / model / VIN]
          Subject:              **URGENT — Notice of Anticipated Litigation; Demand for Preservation of Vehicle and Onboard Data**
5. Greeting — `Dear [Recipient name]:` if known, else `Dear Manager:` or `Dear Risk Manager:` for institutional recipients.

**BODY SECTIONS** (in order):

### I. Notice of Representation and Urgency
One paragraph: this office represents the client identified above. The vehicle described above (or believed to be in the recipient's custody) was involved in a collision on [date of incident] in which our client sustained personal injuries. The vehicle and the data systems on it are evidence of unique and irreplaceable importance. Litigation is reasonably anticipated. State explicitly: 'This letter is URGENT. The vehicle, its systems, and any onboard data must be preserved in their post-collision condition — without repair, alteration, scrapping, parting-out, sale, transfer, auction, or diagnostic interrogation — pending further notice from this office.'

### II. The Duty to Preserve
One paragraph citing:
- Common-law preservation duty under **Silvestri v. General Motors Corp., 271 F.3d 583 (4th Cir. 2001)** — specifically holding that destruction of a vehicle pre-suit can warrant the most severe sanction (dismissal of plaintiff's claim) — and the same principle applies symmetrically to defendants and custodians.
- Federal ESI preservation under **Fed. R. Civ. P. 37(e)** for the digital data resident on the vehicle's EDR, ECM, infotainment, and telematics systems.
- Litigation-hold issuance per **Zubulake v. UBS Warburg LLC, 229 F.R.D. 422 (S.D.N.Y. 2004)** and **ABA Civil Discovery Standards Standard 10**.
- The Sedona Conference Commentary on Legal Holds, 11 Sedona Conf. J. 265 (2010).

### III. Scope of Preservation — Vehicle (Physical)
One paragraph: preserve the vehicle in its post-collision condition. Specifically, **DO NOT**:
- Repair, restore, repaint, replace, or recondition any portion of the vehicle.
- Remove, transfer, sell, salvage, scrap, dismantle, or auction the vehicle.
- Wash, clean, vacuum, or detail the interior or exterior.
- Replace fluids (oil, coolant, brake fluid, transmission fluid, washer fluid).
- Replace tires, wheels, rims, brakes, brake pads, calipers, rotors, lines, or hoses.
- Remove personal property, debris, contents of the cabin, trunk, or cargo area.
- Disable, remove, or replace any airbag module, seat-belt pretensioner, or restraint component.

### IV. Scope of Preservation — Event Data Recorder (EDR / CDR) [include only when includeEDRPreservation=true]
One paragraph: under **49 C.F.R. Part 563 (Event Data Recorders)**, passenger cars and light trucks manufactured on or after September 1, 2012, are equipped with EDR modules that capture pre-crash, crash, and post-crash data — including speed, braking, throttle position, steering angle, restraint deployment, and seat-belt status — typically in a continuously-overwritten buffer that is locked at the moment of airbag deployment. This data is **uniquely time-perishable**: a single attempted engine start, diagnostic interrogation, OBD-II port access, battery disconnect, or repair cycle can corrupt the buffer or lock additional events over the subject crash data. Specifically, **DO NOT**:
- Connect any diagnostic tool, OBD-II reader, or scan tool to the vehicle.
- Disconnect, remove, or replace the vehicle battery.
- Attempt to start the engine, key the ignition, or cycle the electrical system.
- Remove, replace, or interrogate the airbag control module (ACM), restraint control module (RCM), or sensing diagnostic module (SDM).
Reserve all rights to non-destructive imaging of the EDR data using a Bosch Crash Data Retrieval (CDR) tool — or NHTSA-compliant equivalent — by a qualified analyst in the presence of all parties.

### V. Scope of Preservation — ECM, Telematics, ELD [include only when includeECMTelematicsPreservation=true]
One paragraph (commercial / fleet emphasis): for commercial motor vehicles and fleet vehicles, preserve in addition:
- **Engine Control Module (ECM)** data, including the last-stop record, hard-brake events, fault codes, and trip data.
- **Electronic Logging Device (ELD)** records under **49 C.F.R. Part 395** — hours-of-service, driving status, duty status changes, location pings.
- **Telematics provider data** (e.g., Geotab, Samsara, Verizon Connect, Omnitracs, ISAAC) — speed, location, hard-acceleration / hard-braking events, idling, route history, driver-behavior scores. Place a litigation hold on the telematics provider directly.
- **Driver Vehicle Inspection Reports (DVIR)** under **49 C.F.R. § 396.11**, maintenance and repair records under **§ 396.3**, and Driver Qualification File under **§ 391.51**.
- For products-liability investigations, preserve any TSB (technical service bulletin), recall, or campaign records relating to the vehicle's make/model/year.

### VI. Scope of Preservation — Dashcam, Infotainment, Mobile-Phone Pairing [include only when includeDashcamRequest=true OR includeKeyfobAndInfotainmentPreservation=true]
One paragraph: preserve and produce native-format copies of:
- **Dashcam** video and audio (front, rear, cabin, side) — including the buffer cycle covering at least one (1) hour before and one (1) hour after the incident — in native file format (no conversion, redaction, or compression).
- **Infotainment system** records — Bluetooth pairing logs, USB-device pairing, mobile-phone contact-list sync, navigation history, recent-destinations, voice-command history, software/firmware version.
- **Keyfob / smart-key** logs — door unlock/lock events, ignition-cycle history, proximity events.

### VII. Inspection Demand [include only when requestInspection=true]
One paragraph: please confirm the location, current condition, and access procedures for the vehicle. This office requests a joint, non-destructive inspection within **`inspectionWindowDays` (default thirty (30)) days** of this letter, with all parties given fair notice. The inspection will include EDR/CDR imaging by a qualified analyst, photograph documentation, measurement of crush profile and intrusion, and (if relevant) tire/brake/restraint inspection. No party may interrogate the EDR or alter the vehicle outside this joint inspection.

### VIII. Sanctions for Spoliation
One paragraph: this letter places the recipient on notice that any destruction, alteration, repair, sale, or interrogation of the vehicle or its data systems exposes the recipient to:
- Adverse-inference jury instruction.
- Monetary sanctions, attorneys' fees, and costs.
- Preclusion of evidence.
- Under Fed. R. Civ. P. 37(e), where intentional spoliation is found, dispositive sanctions including default judgment and dismissal.
- In some jurisdictions, an independent tort cause of action for spoliation. State explicitly: 'This notice eliminates inadvertence as a defense.'

### IX. Confirmation Request and Closing
One paragraph: please provide written confirmation within **seven (7) days** that (1) the vehicle has been segregated and tagged for preservation, (2) the no-interrogation / no-repair / no-disposal protocol is in place, and (3) a hold has been issued to all employees with access to the vehicle. Direct all communications to undersigned counsel. Please contact this office IMMEDIATELY if there is any pending sale, auction, repair, scrap, or release of the vehicle.

Closing: `Very truly yours,` (suppress when stationery signature block is applied).

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Do NOT cite a specific state spoliation cause-of-action, jury instruction, or sanctions framework unless JURISDICTION CONTEXT supplies it.
- Do NOT include EDR/Part 563 language for vehicle types where the rule does not apply (motorcycles, heavy trucks above 8,500 lbs GVWR pre-2014); for motorcycles, suppress Section IV entirely.
- Do NOT recommend the recipient perform a CDR download themselves — that may corrupt the data and prejudice the client. Always frame as 'reserve the right to non-destructive imaging in a joint inspection.'
- For any disabled `documentOptions` value, suppress the corresponding section entirely.
- If firm stationery is applied, SUPPRESS the `Very truly yours,` closing and signature block.
- Total body text: 700–1,400 words. Target page count: 2–3 pages.
hints: Vehicle / EDR preservation letter — vehicle-specific extension of preservation_letter_pi. Time-critical: tow yards auction within days; EDR buffers can be corrupted by a single OBD-II diagnostic. Cite 49 C.F.R. Part 563 (EDR rule, passenger cars/light trucks 9/1/2012+); for commercial vehicles add 49 C.F.R. Part 395 (ELD), § 396.11 (DVIR), § 396.3 (maintenance), § 391.51 (DQ file), and litigation hold on the telematics provider. Reserve right to joint, non-destructive CDR imaging — never recommend recipient download themselves. Send via certified mail + hand-delivery + email. 2–3 pages.

verificationSources.citation: 49 C.F.R. Part 395 (Hours of Service of Drivers / ELD Mandate)
Silvestri v. General Motors Corp., 271 F.3d 583 (4th Cir. 2001)
Fed. R. Civ. P. 37(e) (Failure to Preserve Electronically Stored Information)
Zubulake v. UBS Warburg LLC, 229 F.R.D. 422 (S.D.N.Y. 2004) (Zubulake V)

knownLimitations: EDR (49 C.F.R. Part 563) applies to passenger cars and light trucks manufactured on or after September 1, 2012; some 2013-and-later light trucks above 8,500 lbs GVWR are excluded. Heavy commercial trucks have separate ECM/ELD regimes — use the ECM/Telematics section instead of the EDR section.

Motorcycles and most pre-2013 vehicles do not have a Part 563-compliant EDR; some have proprietary modules (e.g., Harley-Davidson). Suppress the EDR section for motorcycles and confirm module type before final send.

EDR data preservation requires immediate non-interrogation discipline — every ignition-cycle, OBD-II connection, or battery disconnect risks overwriting the buffer. Recommend joint, non-destructive CDR imaging by a qualified analyst.
Telematics-provider data (Geotab, Samsara, Verizon Connect, Omnitracs, ISAAC) is held by a third party — a separate hold letter to the provider may be required.
Some state vehicle-records statutes (e.g., NY VTL § 416-c) restrict third-party EDR access without owner consent — defer state-specific consent issues to JURISDICTION CONTEXT.
Tow-yard storage liens (state-specific) may limit preservation if the client's vehicle is auctioned for non-payment of storage fees — coordinate with the property-damage adjuster on lien resolution.
Repair-facility recipients are typically NOT defendants — frame the request collaboratively, not adversarially, to maximize cooperation.
